license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.21203/rs.3.pex-1918/v2
title: Multivariate screening of Brugia spp. adults
author: Nicolas J. Wheeler

steps_raw.instruction: On day of receipt, prepare 24-well plates with 750 μL complete media per well (RPMI + 10% FBS + P/S). Ensure media and plates are warmed prior to parasite arrival.
Upon receipt, place the tubes of parasites in the 37°C CO 2 incubator. Parasites typically arrive cold and sluggish but will resume normal motility after 30-60 min. of incubation.
After parasites have warmed, transfer a single parasite to each well of the prepared 24-well plates. Return the plate to the incubator immediately after it is filled with parasites and note the time.
Prepare new 24-well plates with incomplete media (no FBS).
If drug treatments are planned, prepare wells with 750 μL minus the amount of drug to be added. For example, add 675 μL if a 10X drug dilution is planned.
Prepare drug stocks at 10X concentration in RPMI. Keep warm.

Transfer parasites to the new plates ~23 hr after the noted time from Day 1 and return the parasites to 37°C to recover for ~1 hr. Keep the old plates with conditioned media from females (for mf quantification); discard the conditioned media from males.
Transfer all the conditioned media from females to labelled 1.5 mL tubes.
Centrifuge the tubes for 5 min at 10,000 x g .
Discard 500 μL of the supernatant. Store the remainder (pellet of mf, should be ~200 μL) at 4°C.
Video record the plate immediately prior to adding drug.
We typically record the entire plate for 15 s. at 16 FPS using a setup similar to (1).
Immediately after recording, transfer to the BSC and add drug at the desired dilution.
Immediately record.
Return to the incubator.
Record videos again after 1 hr incubation.
Prepare new 24-well plates with incomplete media + 1X drug. Warm plates at 37°C.
Record videos at the 24 hr mark.
Transfer parasites to new plates and return them to the incubator. Keep the plates with conditioned media.
Transfer all the conditioned media from females to labelled 1.5 mL tubes ( Note : male conditioned media does not need to be centrifuged).
Centrifuge the tubes for 5 min at 10,000 x g .
Transfer 500 μL of the supernatant (or conditioned media from males) to a 96-well deep well plate, cover the plate with foil sealing film, and store at -80°C.
For female conditioned media, store the pellet at 4°C. Discard the remaining male conditioned media.
Repeat steps 1-7 on day 4.
For mf pellets stored at 4°C, pipette to mix and transfer 50 μL to a 96-well plate used for the ImageXpress.
Image the plate with 4X, tiling a 2x2 grid to image the entire well. Merge the images after exportation.
Load the images from the entire plate into ImageJ (2) as a stack.
Draw circle ROI and make sure it doesn’t contain the well rim for any images
Edit > Clear outside
Process > Find edges
Image > Adjust > Threshold (dark background, stack histogram) > Apply (unselect everything)
Manually choose a percentage on the histogram that maximizes segmented objects and minimizes background.
Process > Binary > Make binary (unselect everything)
Analyze > Set measurements (Only select area & limit to threshold)
Image > Stacks > Measure stack
Save data as a CSV
Note: Worms that lay less than 200 mf (160,000 pixels) over the first day of recovery can be removed from all downstream data analysis.
Details for the motility, metabolism, and viability analyses can be found in the associated manuscript.

Multivariate chemogenomic screening prioritizes new macrofilaricidal leads by Nicolas J. Wheeler, Kaetlyn T. Ryan, Kendra J. Gallo, Clair R. Henthorn, Spencer S. Ericksen, John D. Chan, Mostafa Zamanian https://dx.doi.org/10.1101/2022.07.25.501423 10.1101/2022.07.25.501423
